title: Render sessions without opaque routing keys
author: RomneyDa
body:
## Summary

Windows session navigation renders opaque Gateway routing keys and treats evolving key suffixes as a fixed schema.

## Observed behavior

Session titles and truncation tooltips can show values such as:

- `agent:main:telegram:main:direct:<peer>`
- `agent:main:tui-<uuid>:heartbeat`

The current parser reads only part of the Gateway session row, expects legacy property names for provider/group metadata, and title formatting assumes `agent:<agent>:<slot>`.

## Expected behavior

- Prefer the Gateway session presentation contract when available.
- Fall back to bounded, human-readable titles for older Gateways without showing full peer IDs, UUIDs, hashes, or routing keys.
- Treat the raw key as an opaque routing value kept for copy/details and actions.
- Group using structured agent metadata.
- Hide cron, heartbeat, dreaming, subagent, ACP, hook, harness, and internal sessions from ordinary conversation navigation by default, with an explicit background-session view.

## Impact

Affected: Windows app users with generated, channel, subagent, automation, or multi-agent sessions.

Severity: Medium usability issue.

Frequency: Consistent whenever unnamed generated sessions are returned.

Consequence: users cannot identify conversations reliably and background execution rows crowd the chat/session navigation.

## Evidence

Observed in the Windows app session selector. Source audit confirms the formatter uses fixed colon positions and the Gateway parser ignores the current `label`, `modelProvider`, `groupChannel`, worktree/node, lineage, and presentation fields.

title: Reasoning effort can't be restored after selecting "off" on Claude Opus 4.7 (gateway rejects every non-off level with "use off")
author: karkarl
body:
## Summary

On `github-copilot/claude-opus-4.7`, once a session's reasoning effort (thinkingLevel) is set to **off**, there is no way to switch it back to any reasoning level. Every non-`off` value the UI sends (`medium`, `max`, …) is rejected by the gateway with `... is not supported for github-copilot/claude-opus-4.7 (use off)`. The model appeared to reason before `off` was selected, so this reads as a one-way trap.

This is **independent of the WinUI chat-composer redesign** — it reproduces on the latest tip of `main` and is a gateway/model-metadata + session-state issue, not a composer-layout issue. Filing separately so it isn't bundled into the composer PR.

## Environment
- App: OpenClaw Tray (WinUI 3), latest `main`
- Gateway: setup-managed local WSL gateway, server version **2026.6.11**
- Model: `github-copilot/claude-opus-4.7` (provider `github-copilot`, api `anthropic-messages`)

## Steps to reproduce
1. Configure a session on `github-copilot/claude-opus-4.7`.
2. Open the reasoning-effort picker and select **off** (e.g. while exploring the UI).
3. Try to switch the reasoning effort back to `medium` (or any other level).

## Expected
The user can restore the model's previous/default reasoning behavior (either a working reasoning level, or a clearly-labeled "default"/"on" state).

## Actual
The switch silently fails; the session stays on `off`. The gateway rejects the patch:

```
sessions.patch failed: thinkingLevel "medium" is not supported for github-copilot/claude-opus-4.7 (use off)
sessions.patch failed: thinkingLevel "max"    is not supported for github-copilot/claude-opus-4.7 (use off)
```

There is no exposed path back to the model's default reasoning state — the only gateway-accepted explicit value is `off`.

## Evidence: contradictory model metadata

`models.list` reports this model as **non-reasoning** yet simultaneously advertises a full effort list:

```json
{
  "id": "claude-opus-4.7",
  "name": "Claude Opus 4.7",
  "provider": "github-copilot",
  "api": "anthropic-messages",
  "contextWindow": 128000,
  "reasoning": false,
  "compat": { "supportedReasoningEfforts": ["low","medium","high","xhigh","max"] },
  "available": true
}
```

So `reasoning: false` and `compat.supportedReasoningEfforts: [low,medium,high,xhigh,max]` disagree, and the `sessions.patch` validator only accepts `off`. A UI cannot trust the advertised effort list to decide what is selectable.

## Analysis / open questions

Two candidate root causes (need gateway-side confirmation):

1. **One-way "off" trap.** The model's *default* (unset thinkingLevel) may be a working reasoning state, and setting an explicit `off` leaves no way to return to that default — there's no `default`/`unset`/`on` value the UI can send, and `off` is the only accepted explicit value. If so, the gateway and/or UI needs a way to clear/restore the default reasoning state.
2. **Stale/incorrect `reasoning` metadata** for Copilot-hosted Anthropic models — if Opus 4.7 truly supports reasoning through Copilot, `reasoning: false` (and the patch validator's `use off`) is wrong and should be corrected so real efforts are accepted.

## Impact
- Users can permanently (per session) lose reasoning on a model after a single stray toggle, with no visible recovery path.
- The contradictory `reasoning` vs `supportedReasoningEfforts` metadata makes it impossible for the client to present an accurate, gate-correct reasoning picker.

## Notes for the client-side follow-up
- A partial client-side mitigation was prototyped (gate the reasoning picker on the `reasoning` flag and disable unsupported efforts instead of letting them silently fail). That improves honesty but does **not** solve the core problem — restoring reasoning after `off` — because the gateway still accepts only `off` for this model. The real fix depends on resolving the open questions above (a "default/restore" path and/or correct metadata).

title: Gateway-wrapped commands bypass usable Windows exec allow rules
author: steipete
body:
## Problem

Gateway node execution on Windows sends commands through the platform shell as an argv vector such as `cmd.exe /d /s /c hostname`. The Windows node formats that vector into one command string, evaluates the outer wrapper against the host exec policy, and returns on the default deny before `ExecShellWrapperParser` can evaluate the inner command.

This makes default low-risk allow rules such as the exact `hostname` rule unusable through the real Gateway `exec host=node` path even though direct Windows-node policy evaluation allows the inner command.

## Reproduction

1. Connect Windows Hub 0.6.12 as a node to a current OpenClaw Gateway.
2. Confirm the host-native policy has the default exact `hostname` allow rule.
3. Ask an agent to run exactly `hostname` through `exec` with `host=node`.
4. Observe `Command denied by exec policy`.

The Gateway-generated argv is `cmd.exe /d /s /c hostname`. The host policy denies that wrapper before expanding it to the allowed nested target.

## Expected

A recognized shell wrapper should be allowed when every parsed inner target is allowed, while malformed wrappers and explicit deny rules on either the wrapper or any nested target remain fail-closed.

## Scope

This is a narrow Windows host exec-policy ordering bug. It does not change the broader Windows-vs-WSL command/path product contract tracked in #911.

title: Windows Companion: "Agent thinking" animation closes the chat/session selectbox before a different chat can be chosen
author: danilovmy
body: When the animated `Agent thinking` indicator is visible, it becomes difficult or impossible to switch to another chat from the selectbox because the animation keeps triggering UI updates/repaints and the selectbox closes before the user can complete the selection. In practice this makes switching chats unreliable or impossible while the animation is active. ## Why this matters Switching chats while one agent is still thinking is a normal workflow. The animated status should not steal focus or destabilize the session/chat picker. ## Repro 1. Open Windows Companion. 2. Start or wait for a chat where the UI shows the animated `Agent thinking` label. 3. Open the chat/session selectbox. 4. Try to choose another chat while the animation is still running. In practice this makes switching chats unreliable or impossible while the animation is active. ## Expected - The selectbox should stay open long enough for the user to pick another chat. - The `Agent thinking` animation can continue visually, but it must not reset/close the open dropdown. - Ongoing animated status updates should not interfere with normal pointer/keyboard selection in the chat picker. ## Actual - The screen keeps repainting while `Agent thinking` animates. - The selectbox closes before the selection can be completed. ## Suggested fix - Ensure the `Agent thinking` animation does not remount or reset the chat/session picker. - Keep the picker open state stable across unrelated animation/status updates.

title: Windows Companion try to resolve paths on the local machine for remote agent Workspace
author: danilovmy
body: ## Summary When Windows Companion is connected to a remote agent/gateway, the `Advanced -> Agents -> main -> Workspace` view does not show the expected files because it appears to resolve/browse paths against the local Windows machine instead of the remote agent workspace. ## Why this matters The whole point of the remote Workspace view is to inspect the remote agent's workspace/files. If the UI tries to interpret those paths locally, the file rail/browser becomes misleading or empty for legitimate remote paths. ## Repro 1. Connect Windows Companion to a remote agent/gateway. 2. Open `Advanced -> Agents -> main -> Workspace`. 3. Expect to browse the remote agent workspace/files. 4. Observe that the view does not show the expected files and behaves as if the paths are being resolved on the local Windows machine. ## Expected - Workspace browsing should be scoped to the connected remote agent/gateway. - File listing and preview should use the remote workspace APIs and remote path semantics. - Remote absolute paths should never be treated as local Windows filesystem paths just because the UI is running on Windows. ## Actual - The Workspace tab does not show the expected remote files. - Behavior suggests local path resolution / local-machine assumptions rather than remote workspace-backed browsing. ## Notes / suspected shape This looks related to the existing Workspace/file-rail work: - `#876 Add Workspace session file rail` - `#871 Follow up Workspace file rail parity gaps` - `#956 Fix workspace file previews` Especially `#956` looks adjacent because it explicitly mentions routing the Workspace page through: - `agents.workspace.list` - `agents.workspace.get` with fallback behavior for older gateways. So this may be: 1. a regression in the remote-workspace path, 2. an incomplete remote-agent case not covered by the earlier fix, 3. or a fallback path still using local/path-based assumptions when remote `agents.workspace.*` should be authoritative. ## Suggested fix - Ensure the Workspace page uses remote `agents.workspace.list/get` results as canonical when connected to a remote agent/gateway. - Do not reinterpret remote paths as local Windows paths in the UI layer. - Audit fallback order so older-gateway fallbacks still preserve remote scope instead of accidentally dropping into local-machine browsing semantics. - Add an explicit regression test for a remote/non-Windows-style workspace root.

title: No in-app way to reconfigure model/API key for a setup-managed local WSL gateway
author: karkarl
body:
## Summary

When the active gateway is a **setup-managed local WSL gateway** (e.g. `OpenClawGateway` / `OpenClawGateway-Dev`), there is **no in-app way to re-run the "OpenClaw onboard" step** to fix a wrong model or a bad/expired API key. The user is stuck with whatever provider/model/key they entered during first-run setup.

## Repro

1. Complete first-run setup with the default **Install a local gateway (WSL)** flow.
2. During the gateway-driven **OpenClaw onboard** step, pick a wrong model and/or enter an invalid API key (easy to do by rushing through).
3. Open the chat — the model fails (auth/model error).
4. Try to fix it from the app: right-click the tray icon looking for **Reconfigure**.

## Expected

There should be a discoverable in-app action to re-run the provider/model/key onboarding (or otherwise change the model + credentials) against the existing local WSL gateway.

## Actual

The tray **"Reconfigure" / "Setup Guide"** entry is **not shown**. It is intentionally suppressed for setup-managed local WSL gateways:

- `App.xaml.cs` → `CaptureTrayMenuSnapshot()`:
  ```csharp
  var hasSetupManagedLocalWslGateway = WslKeepAlivePolicy.HasSetupManagedLocalGateway(_gatewayRegistry?.GetAll());
  ...
  ShowSetupMenuEntry = !hasSetupManagedLocalWslGateway,
  ```
- `TrayMenuStateBuilder.cs` only adds the `"setup"` menu item when `_snapshot.ShowSetupMenuEntry` is true.

The model + API key live **inside the WSL gateway** (OpenClaw's own config, e.g. `~/.openclaw[-dev]/openclaw.json`), configured by the gateway-driven `wizard.*` onboard flow — not in the Hub's `settings.json`. So the Hub has no settings surface for them either.

In a packaged install the documented fallback is the **Start Menu → "OpenClaw Gateway Setup"** shortcut (`docs/SETUP.md`), but that shortcut is not present in every context (e.g. isolated/dev runs, or users who don't know to look there), and it's not discoverable from the failure point (the chat).

## Current workaround

Reconfigure directly in the gateway distro:

```powershell
wsl -d OpenClawGateway -- bash -lc "openclaw configure --section model"
# dev-profile gateway:
wsl -d OpenClawGateway-Dev -- bash -lc "openclaw --dev configure --section model"
```

This requires dropping to a WSL shell and knowing the `--dev` profile nuance — not acceptable as the only path for a non-technical user.

## Proposed fix (options)

1. **Surface a "Reconfigure gateway" / "Run OpenClaw onboard" action for setup-managed local WSL gateways.** Either re-show the tray Setup/Reconfigure entry for this gateway type (routing into the existing onboard wizard against the running gateway), or add an equivalent button on the **Connection** page for the active local WSL gateway.
2. **Add an error-driven affordance:** when the chat surfaces an auth/model failure, offer a "Fix model / API key" action that routes to the same onboard flow (aligns with the design principle that error states must be legible and route the user to the fix).
3. At minimum, expose the model/credentials section of `openclaw configure` through the setup UI so it doesn't require a manual WSL command.

## Design notes

- Follow the existing onboarding wizard pattern (`docs/ONBOARDING_WIZARD.md`, `docs/SETUP.md`) — reuse the gateway `wizard.*` transcript flow rather than inventing a new form.
- Keep with the design system: route users to the fix visibly instead of a silent no-op; no hard-coded hex; native Fluent surfaces.
- Respect credential/gateway ownership boundaries in `docs/CONNECTION_ARCHITECTURE.md` (gateway registry + device identity), and the `--dev`/profile distinction so the correct gateway state is edited.

## Affected code

- `src/OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI/App.xaml.cs` — `CaptureTrayMenuSnapshot()` (`ShowSetupMenuEntry` gating)
- `src/OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI/Services/TrayMenuStateBuilder.cs` — Setup/Reconfigure menu entry
- `src/OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI/Services/WslKeepAlivePolicy.cs` — `HasSetupManagedLocalGateway`
- `src/OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI/Pages/ConnectionPage.xaml.cs` — candidate host for an in-page reconfigure action
- Onboard flow: `src/OpenClaw.SetupEngine*`, `docs/ONBOARDING_WIZARD.md`, `docs/SETUP.md`

Co-authored-by: Copilot App <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

title: Add opt-in Tailscale Serve with tailnet identity authentication for generated WSL gateways
author: andyeskridge
body:
# Summary
Add an opt-in Tailscale Serve mode when creating or replacing an OpenClaw-generated WSL gateway. The gateway remains loopback-bound inside WSL, while Tailscale Serve publishes private HTTPS/WSS access on the user’s tailnet.
When enabled, the generated gateway should trust Tailscale identity authentication in addition to its normal token/device flows.
## Requested behavior

- Add a setup toggle for Tailnet access with Tailscale Serve.
- Require Windows Tailscale to be installed, connected, and MagicDNS-enabled before destructive gateway replacement begins.
- Install and authorize Tailscale inside the generated WSL distro.
- Support browser authorization and transient auth-key authorization; never persist auth keys. 
- Register Companion with the discovered `wss://<wsl-node>.<tailnet>.ts.net` endpoint while preserving app-managed WSL behavior, keepalive, and replacement detection.
- Use the real discovered tailnet suffix in setup and completion UI.
- Do not silently fall back to localhost for managed `.ts.net` gateways.
- Add Tailscale-specific recovery guidance for Windows login, tailnet mismatch, WSL daemon state, Serve status, and gateway health.
- Redact auth keys and Tailscale authorization URLs from logs, journals, diagnostics, and errors.
- Configure OpenClaw with:
```
gateway.bind=loopback
gateway.tailscale.mode=serve
gateway.tailscale.resetOnExit=false
gateway.auth.allowTailscale=true
```


## Security note
Enabling `gateway.auth.allowTailscale=true` means tailnet ACLs and Tailscale identity become an authorization path for the gateway. The setup UI and documentation should state this clearly. Token and device credentials should remain supported for pairing and compatibility.
## Acceptance criteria

- Windows and WSL must be on the same MagicDNS tailnet before pairing.
- `tailscale serve status --json` routes HTTPS to the loopback gateway port.
- Windows can reach the generated `https://…ts.net` endpoint.
- Companion registers a managed local WSS gateway record and connects through it.
- The Windows node connects through the WSS record.
- `openclaw nodes invoke --command device.info --params '{}'` succeeds through the real gateway.

title: Docs: correct the setup-code expiry contract
author: steipete
body:
## Summary

`docs/gateway-node-integration.md` repeatedly says official setup codes embed expiry metadata, but the Gateway payload and active Windows decoder contain only URL and bootstrap-token fields.

## Current behavior

The guide describes `{ url, bootstrapToken, expiresAtMs }`, says the decoded payload contains expiry, and retains a checklist item for a Windows expiry parser that was removed in #925.

## Expected behavior

Document the upstream payload as `{ url, urls?, bootstrapToken }`, the Windows-consumed shape as `{ url, bootstrapToken }`, and the 10-minute lifetime as Gateway-enforced rather than serialized into the setup code.

## Impact

Windows integration work can incorrectly require or implement a field that official setup codes never contain, or assume the current tray performs a client-side expiry check that no longer exists.

## Evidence

- Upstream payload type and builder omit expiry: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/d395a8e6ec2789763b746b1e96ed5870c7795bd7/src/pairing/setup-code.ts#L37-L41 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/d395a8e6ec2789763b746b1e96ed5870c7795bd7/src/pairing/setup-code.ts#L434-L445
- Gateway token state applies a 10-minute TTL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/d395a8e6ec2789763b746b1e96ed5870c7795bd7/src/infra/device-bootstrap.ts#L25-L26
- Current Windows decoder reads only `url` and `bootstrapToken`: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw-windows-node/blob/053501c6dfaefc17b9110ea5b622713f04a2b753/src/OpenClaw.Connection/SetupCodeDecoder.cs#L47-L77
- The upstream documentation correction landed in openclaw/openclaw#103943.

No open duplicate issue or PR was found.

title: Bug: Chat session names are not unique (fork creates duplicate "OpenClaw Windows Tray")
author: karkarl
body:
## Summary
Chat session names are not guaranteed to be unique. When #952 occurs (the app forks instead of reusing the existing session), it spins up a fork with the **same name** as the original — e.g. two sessions both named `OpenClaw Windows Tray`. This makes sessions indistinguishable in the list.

## Expected
Session names are unique. A forked/new session cannot collide with an existing session's name.

## Actual
Forking (see #952) produces a duplicate session with an identical name (`OpenClaw Windows Tray`), so users can't tell them apart.

## Acceptance criteria
- Session names are unique across active sessions.
- Creating/forking a session that would collide with an existing name disambiguates it (e.g. suffix/counter or a distinct generated name).
- Duplicates are visually distinguishable in the session list.

## Related
- #952 — fork-instead-of-reuse bug that triggers the duplicate name.

## Context
Surfaced during chat-session review (2026-07-09).

title: Sort & filter chat sessions in the companion app by recency, isIdle, isCompleted
author: karkarl
body:
## Summary
Add sorting and filtering to the chat session list in the Windows companion app.

## Details
Users should be able to:
- **Sort** the session list by most-recent activity (recency).
- **Filter** by session state: `isIdle`, `isCompleted`.

## Acceptance criteria
- Session list can be sorted by recency.
- Session list can be filtered by idle and completed state.

## Related
- #881 (completed ACP/subagent sessions clutter the list — hide/archive support).

## Context
Surfaced during chat-session review (2026-07-08).

title: Bug: Windows companion app may create a new fork instead of reusing the existing session
author: karkarl
body:
## Summary
The Windows companion app appears to sometimes create a new **forked** session instead of attaching to / reusing the existing session.

## Expected
Opening or continuing a session reuses the existing session. Forking only happens on explicit user action.

## Actual
A new fork may be created in place of the existing session (session-resolution path picks/creates a new session).

## Next steps
- Reproduce and capture the conditions that trigger the unwanted fork.
- Root-cause the session-resolution / attach path.

## Acceptance criteria
- Continuing an existing session reuses it (no unintended fork).
- Forking occurs only when the user explicitly requests it.

## Context
Surfaced during chat-session review (2026-07-08).

title: Chat composer + chat UI should scale to large chat session lists
author: karkarl
body: ## Summary The chat composer and chat UI need to scale to a large number of chat sessions without degrading performance or breaking layout. ## Details As the number of chat sessions grows, the session list and surrounding chat UI should remain responsive. This likely means virtualizing the session list and ensuring the composer + timeline don't degrade with high session counts. Example: ## Acceptance criteria - Session list stays smooth/responsive with hundreds of sessions. - No layout breakage at high session counts. - Composer and chat timeline remain usable throughout. ## Context Surfaced during design-system / chat UX work (2026-07-08).

title: After close to tray and open again list of sessions ALWAYS set on middle of list and not on last chat.
author: danilovmy
body: windows 10. Companion, Native chat. I start the companion, after opening chat. In select box with sessions list the session from middle of list is choosen. Start: chat open, automatically choosen session with **bonsai light**. I choose "reciept main session" In this chat i perform my tasks succefully. But suddenly i need to switch on other app in windows. I close chat with "X", because i dont want to see minimized tab on task panel. But the companion still works: Next chat opening -> In select box with "sessions list" the session from middle of list is choosen, not the last active chat. Awaited: Last active chat is open. or setting, where i can say - which chat should be opened by default.

title: Add DevBuild identity marker check to CI
author: shanselman
body:
The stabilization PR locally proved `dotnet build src\OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI -c Debug -r win-x64 -p:DevBuild=true --no-restore` produces `app-identity.txt` containing `dev`, but the CI workflow change could not be pushed from the agent because the current OAuth token lacks GitHub `workflow` scope.

Add a CI step after the main WinUI/UI validation that:

```powershell
dotnet build src/OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI -c Debug -r win-x64 -p:DevBuild=true --no-restore
$marker = Get-ChildItem -Path src\OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI\bin\Debug -Recurse -Filter app-identity.txt |
  Where-Object { $_.FullName -like '*\win-x64\app-identity.txt' } |
  Sort-Object LastWriteTimeUtc -Descending |
  Select-Object -First 1
if ($null -eq $marker) { throw "Dev identity marker was not produced by the DevBuild." }
$identity = (Get-Content -LiteralPath $marker.FullName -Raw).Trim()
if ($identity -ne "dev") { throw "Expected DevBuild identity marker 'dev' but found '$identity' at $($marker.FullName)." }
```

This is the remaining follow-up to move confidence beyond the current ~95% main baseline.

title: Bug: user chat messages may render double
author: calebeden
body: While attempting to reproduce #899, I ran into a scenario where the app started rendering user messages twice. After doing this for a few messages, it went back to working normally for subsequent messages. The messages only appear once when I looked through the gateway web dashboard, and after relaunching the companion app, they now only show once as expected. The agent only ever saw a single copy of each message, so this is a rendering issue in the app.

title: Gateway commands mix Linux vs Windows semantics + path confusion
author: samanthamsong
body:
**Problem:** Gateway commands **mix Linux vs Windows semantics** and there is **path confusion** between the WSL and Windows sides. (Related to but distinct from #901, which tracks WSL Gateway <-> Tray version lock-step.)

**Impact:** Platform/infra - confusing, error-prone command surface across WSL/Windows.

**Epic:** should ladder up to #834 (S1 - Local gateway golden path).

title: No documentation; "operator vs node" concepts unclear
author: samanthamsong
body:
**Problem:** There is **no documentation**, and the **"operator vs node"** concepts are unclear to new users.

**Impact:** Onboarding - users can't self-serve; concept confusion.

**Epic:** should ladder up to #834 (S1 - Local gateway golden path).

title: No approval UX when a new node requests pairing
author: samanthamsong
body:
**Source:** OpenClaw Windows Node Bug Bash (2026-06-30). Reported by Scott Hanselman.

**Problem:** There is **no approval UX** when a new node requests pairing - the user isn't prompted to approve/deny a new pairing request.

**Impact:** Pairing / security - missing consent step for new nodes.

**Epic:** should ladder up to #835 (S2 - Connect & pair).
